text: Hello.
zh: 你好。

text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Georgie.
zh: 我是乔治。

text: In winter, people find different ways of keeping warm, like wearing extra clothes or staying indoors.
zh: 冬天,人们会想出各种办法保暖,比如多穿衣服或待在室内。

text: But in some countries, there's another way of keeping the cold out – going for a sauna.
zh: 但在一些国家,还有另一种驱寒的方式——去蒸桑拿。

text: Yes.
zh: 没错。

text: Nordic countries like Sweden and Finland have a long history of taking saunas, but now they're popping up in Britain too.
zh: 像瑞典、芬兰这样的北欧国家有着悠久的桑拿传统,但现在英国也开始流行起来了。

text: Have you ever been for a sauna, Georgie?
zh: 乔治,你去蒸过桑拿吗?

text: I have, yes, and if I'm honest, I'm not a huge fan.
zh: 去过,说实话,我不太喜欢。

text: I don't really like being too hot.
zh: 我不太喜欢太热的感觉。

text: It's uncomfortable.
zh: 觉得很难受。

text: What about you, Neil?
zh: 你呢,尼尔?

text: Oh, really?
zh: 哦,真的吗?

text: I quite like a sauna.
zh: 我还挺喜欢桑拿的。

text: You feel really refreshed afterwards.
zh: 蒸完之后感觉特别神清气爽。

text: So, what is a sauna?
zh: 那么,桑拿到底是什么呢?

text: Well, a typical sauna is a small wooden room heated with steam to around 80 degrees Celsius.
zh: 一个典型的桑拿房是一个用蒸汽加热到大约80摄氏度的小木屋。

text: It's hot enough to make anyone sweat.
zh: 这个温度足以让任何人大汗淋漓。

text: But are saunas good for your health as well?
zh: 但桑拿对健康也有好处吗?

text: that's what James Gallagher wanted to find out for BBC Radio 4 programme, Inside Health.
zh: 这正是詹姆斯·加拉格尔为BBC第四台节目《Inside Health》想要弄清楚的问题。

text: We're seeing what saunas do to the human body.
zh: 我们要看看桑拿对人体到底有什么影响。

text: They're popping up all over the country with claims that high temperatures boost your health and wellbeing.
zh: 桑拿正在全国各地兴起,人们声称高温能提升健康和幸福感。

text: But are they all they're cracked up to be?
zh: 但它们真的名副其实吗?

text: James wants to find out if saunas are all they're cracked up to be.
zh: 詹姆斯想弄清楚桑拿是否真的名副其实。

text: He means, are they as good as people say?
zh: 他的意思是,桑拿是否真如人们所说的那么好。

text: And that's exactly what we'll be finding out in this episode, along with some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 这正是我们这期节目要探讨的内容,同时还会学到一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: And remember, you can practice all the new vocabulary from this episode with the quiz and worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住,你可以通过我们网站bbclearningenglish.com上的测验和练习题来巩固这期节目学到的词汇。

text: But now I have a question for you, Georgie.
zh: 不过现在我有个问题要问你,乔治。

text: The word sauna comes from the Finnish language, but what does it mean?
zh: sauna这个词来自芬兰语,但它的意思是什么呢?

text: Does it mean a sweat, b hot water or c bathhouse?
zh: 是a出汗,b热水,还是c浴室?

text: I'm going to guess c bathhouse.
zh: 我猜是c浴室。

text: OK, well we'll find out if you're right at the end of the programme.
zh: 好,我们节目最后会揭晓答案,看你猜得对不对。

text: In recent winters, saunas have grown in popularity in the UK.
zh: 最近几个冬天,桑拿在英国越来越流行。

text: BBC reporter James Gallagher met some people going to Moor a Sauna, a beach sauna in South Wales.
zh: BBC记者詹姆斯·加拉格尔在南威尔士一家海滩桑拿房Moor a Sauna遇到了一些常客。

text: He asked them why they love saunas so much.
zh: 他问他们为什么这么喜欢桑拿。

text: Hello everyone!
zh: 大家好!

text: Hello!
zh: 你好!

text: Who loves a sauna?
zh: 谁喜欢桑拿?

text: Me!
zh: 我!

text: Tell me why.
zh: 说说为什么吧。

text: Oh, it's just so relaxing.
zh: 哦,这让人特别放松。

text: It just makes you feel at ease.
zh: 它能让你感到很自在。

text: It's also great for relaxing muscles if you're up tense or anything like that at all.
zh: 如果你肌肉紧张什么的,桑拿对放松肌肉也很有帮助。

text: Yeah, I always love coming down here when I'm a bit stressed out.
zh: 是啊,每次压力大的时候我都爱来这儿。

text: And by the time that I'm finished, I feel like I've completely reset.
zh: 等我蒸完出来,感觉整个人都焕然一新了。

text: It's wonderful.
zh: 太棒了。

text: The first speaker says saunas make her feel at ease, a phrase meaning comfortable and relaxed.
zh: 第一位说话者说桑拿让她感到at ease,这个短语的意思是舒适、放松。

text: And the next speaker thinks saunas are great if you feel tense, an adjective meaning worried and unable to relax.
zh: 下一位说话者认为,如果你感到tense(紧张),桑拿会很有帮助,这个形容词的意思是担忧、无法放松。

text: Saunas make the final speaker feel like she's reset.
zh: 桑拿让最后一位说话者感觉像reset(重启)了一样。

text: You might already know the word reset to mean turning a computer off and on again when it's not working,
zh: 你可能已经知道reset这个词,指电脑出故障时关机再开机,

text: but used in connection with a person, reset means to start again after a rest so that you feel better about life.
zh: 但用在人身上时,reset指的是经过休息后重新开始,让你对生活感觉更好。

text: So, it seems saunas do help people feel better, but is there actual medical evidence that it's good for us?
zh: 看来桑拿确实能让人感觉更好,但有没有实际的医学证据证明它对我们有益呢?

text: Here's James Gallagher again speaking with Professor Damian Bailey, an expert in human physiology for BBC Radio 4 programme Inside Health.
zh: 下面还是詹姆斯·加拉格尔与人体生理学专家戴米安·贝利教授的对话,来自BBC第四台节目《Inside Health》。

text: When we start to connect this to the arguments around health benefits, is there a health benefit to going in a sauna?
zh: 当我们把这个和健康益处联系起来时,蒸桑拿对健康真的有好处吗?

text: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

text: I mean, I looked at the research really closely.
zh: 我仔细研究过相关的研究。

text: I was fascinated by the evidence.
zh: 这些证据让我十分着迷。

text: huge benefits, I mean really huge benefits.
zh: 益处巨大,我是说真的非常巨大。

text: And it's cultural of course, 5 million people in Finland, 90 % of them use saunas.
zh: 这当然也和文化有关,芬兰有500万人口,其中90%的人都会去蒸桑拿。

text: Maybe we should be copying what the Finns do, because there's a 40 % reduction in all -cause mortality with the sauna goers.
zh: 也许我们应该效仿芬兰人的做法,因为蒸桑拿的人全因死亡率降低了40%。

text: Now this is what we would call observational data, but it's based on thousands and thousands of sauna goers.
zh: 这就是我们所说的观察性数据,但它基于成千上万蒸桑拿者的数据。

text: These are observational data sets, so we really do need what we call RCTs, randomized controlled trials, so everything, just as you've mentioned there, everything is absolutely controlled.
zh: 这些是观察性数据集,所以我们确实需要所谓的RCT,也就是随机对照试验,让一切都像你刚才提到的那样,完全受控。

text: But we can't turn our noses up at the current data, the observational data.
zh: 但我们也不能对现有的观察性数据嗤之以鼻。

text: Damian discusses the evidence from Finland, a country where 90 % of the population are regular sauna -goers.
zh: 戴米安讨论了来自芬兰的证据,那里90%的人口经常蒸桑拿。

text: And it's impressive – a 40 % reduction in deaths from all causes.
zh: 这个数据令人印象深刻——全因死亡率降低了40%。

text: But it's important to remember that this is based on observational data – information collected from watching people's natural behaviour and not from controlled medical trials.
zh: 但要记住,这是基于观察性数据的——也就是通过观察人们的自然行为收集的信息,而不是通过对照医学试验。

text: Nevertheless, this observational data is based on evidence from thousands and thousands of Finns over many years.
zh: 尽管如此,这些观察性数据来自多年间成千上万芬兰人的证据。

text: That's why Damien thinks we shouldn't turn our noses up at it.
zh: 这就是为什么戴米安认为我们不该对它嗤之以鼻。

text: We shouldn't reject it simply because we think it's not good enough for us.
zh: 我们不应该仅仅因为觉得它不够好就直接否定它。

text: Well, all this talk about saunas has made me want to give it another try.
zh: 好吧,听了这么多关于桑拿的讨论,我也想再试一次了。

text: But not before you reveal the answer to your question, Neil.
zh: 不过在那之前,尼尔,你得先揭晓你那个问题的答案。

text: I asked you what the Finnish word sauna means.
zh: 我问你,芬兰语单词sauna是什么意思。

text: Is it a sweat, b hot water or c bathhouse?
zh: 是a出汗,b热水,还是c浴室?

text: And I said bathhouse, am I right?
zh: 我说的是浴室,我说对了吗?

text: You are absolutely correct.
zh: 你说得完全正确。

text: Well done.
zh: 太棒了。

text: Yay!
zh: 耶!

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned in this episode.
zh: 好,让我们来回顾一下这期节目学到的词汇。

text: If something is not all it's cracked up to be, it isn't as good as people say.
zh: 如果某事not all it's cracked up to be,意思是它并不像人们说的那么好。

text: When someone's at ease, they feel comfortable and unstressed, whereas a tense person is worried and unable to relax.
zh: 当一个人at ease时,他们感到舒适、无压力;而tense的人则是担忧、无法放松。

text: When someone resets, they start again after a rest, which has made them feel better about life.
zh: 当一个人reset时,他们经过休息后重新开始,感觉对生活更有信心了。

text: Observational data is information collected from watching people's natural behaviour without interfering in it.
zh: observational data(观察性数据)是指通过观察人们的自然行为、而不加以干预所收集到的信息。

text: And finally, if you turn your nose up at something, you reject it because you think it's not good enough for you.
zh: 最后,如果你turn your nose up at 某事,意思是你因为觉得它不够好而拒绝它。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you want to improve your English with more trending topics and useful vocabulary, you'll find plenty on our website, BBC Learning English dot com.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到了尾声,不过如果你想通过更多热门话题和实用词汇提升英语水平,可以在我们的网站BBC Learning English上找到更多内容。

text: See you again soon, but for now it's goodbye.
zh: 我们很快再见,现在先说再见啦。
